name: Sierra de Altamira Loop from Puerto de San Vicente
name_original: Sierra de Altamira — circular desde Puerto de San Vicente
description: This easy 2.9-mile circular hike from Puerto de San Vicente offers panoramic views and diverse wildlife in the Sierra de Altamira.
region_intro: Hiking trails around La Jara are primarily found within the Cabañeros National Park area in Spain, characterized by its diverse landscapes including river valleys, dense forests, and mountainous terrain. The region features trails along rivers like the Pusa, leading to waterfalls and through areas with significant natural beauty. Elevations vary, offering both gentle paths and more challenging climbs through rugged sections. The area provides a network of trails suitable for exploring the natural environment. Best hiking trails around La Jara The most popular hiking route is Waterfall of los Navalucillos — Cabañeros National Park, a 5.1 miles (8.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 27 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads to a notable waterfall within the national park. Another top favourite among local hikers is Route of the Chorro de los Navalucillos – El Parque Nacional de Cabañeros, a difficult 12.4 miles (19.9 km) path. This trail offers an extended exploration of the national park's rugged terrain and natural features. Local hikers also love the Garganta de las Lanchas Trail, a 5.9 miles (9.5 km) trail leading through a gorge, often completed in about 3 hours 8 minutes. Hiking around La Jara is defined by river paths, waterfall trails, and routes through Cabañeros National Park. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to difficult ascents. The routes in La Jara are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 29 reviews. More than 260 hikers have used komoot to explore La Jara's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of hiking trails can I expect around La Jara?
A: Hiking around La Jara primarily features trails within the Cabañeros National Park area in Spain. You'll find diverse landscapes, including river valleys, dense forests, and mountainous terrain. Many routes follow rivers like the Pusa, leading to waterfalls and through areas of significant natural beauty. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, La Jara offers several easy trails. For a gentle walk, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021127098/malpasillo-bridge-and-estrechura-del-pusa-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Malpasillo Bridge and Estrechura del Pusa Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is just over 4.5 km. Another accessible option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1188118729/ruta-de-la-encina-parque-nacional-de-cabaneros-round-trip" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de la Encina — Parque Nacional de Cabañeros — round trip&lt;/a&gt;, a short 2 km trail perfect for a leisurely stroll.

Q: Can I find trails with waterfalls in the La Jara area?
A: Absolutely! Waterfalls are a prominent feature of the hiking experience here. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018228965/waterfall-of-los-navalucillos-cabaneros-national-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall of los Navalucillos — Cabañeros National Park&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 8.2 km route. For a more challenging adventure, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1085331004/route-of-the-chorro-de-los-navalucillos-el-parque-nacional-de-cabaneros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Chorro de los Navalucillos&lt;/a&gt; offers an extended exploration of the park's natural features, including its impressive waterfall. Don't miss the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1219455524/rocigalgo-and-el-chorro-waterfalls-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rocigalgo and El Chorro Waterfalls Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt; for a challenging hike connecting two major waterfalls.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around La Jara?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available, offering convenient starting and ending points. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1219443515/rio-pusa-from-los-navalucillos-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Pusa from Los Navalucillos — circular&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.5 km loop. For a longer, more challenging option that includes waterfalls, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1219455524/rocigalgo-and-el-chorro-waterfalls-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rocigalgo and El Chorro Waterfalls Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;. Another moderate circular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1218447231/sendero-cerro-el-castrejon-sl-to-02-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero Cerro El Castrejón (SL-TO 02) — circular&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in La Jara?
A: The hiking trails in La Jara are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.8 stars from over 29 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse terrain, the beauty of the Cabañeros National Park, and the presence of stunning waterfalls along the routes.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots to discover on the hikes?
A: Yes, you can find several scenic spots. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1218326195/bellavista-viewpoint-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bellavista Viewpoint Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.6 km hike specifically designed to lead you to a beautiful vista. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5417642" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Azután Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; offers picturesque views, often accessible from nearby trails.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or landmarks near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond the trails themselves, the area boasts several natural monuments. You can visit the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3336126" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorro de Los Navalucillos Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, the majestic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839746" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Rocigalgo&lt;/a&gt; summit, and the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5633420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrera Chica Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3019475" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrera de las Lanchas&lt;/a&gt; is another beautiful waterfall worth exploring.

Q: Are there any historical or man-made attractions to see while hiking?
A: Yes, you can encounter some interesting man-made structures. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3851007" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amador Bridge or Azután Viaduct&lt;/a&gt; is a notable landmark. You might also find charming local architecture in places like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2683470" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcaudete de la Jara Town Square and Monuments&lt;/a&gt;, which can be a pleasant stop after a hike.

Q: How long do hikes in La Jara typically take?
A: Hikes in La Jara vary significantly in duration. Shorter, easy trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1188118729/ruta-de-la-encina-parque-nacional-de-cabaneros-round-trip" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de la Encina&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in under an hour. Moderate routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018228965/waterfall-of-los-navalucillos-cabaneros-national-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall of los Navalucillos&lt;/a&gt;, typically take around 2.5 hours. For more difficult and longer trails, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1085331004/route-of-the-chorro-de-los-navalucillos-el-parque-nacional-de-cabaneros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Chorro de los Navalucillos&lt;/a&gt;, you should plan for a full day, as they can take over 6 hours.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of hiking trails in La Jara?
A: The trails around La Jara offer a good mix of difficulty levels. Out of 19 available routes, you'll find 4 easy trails perfect for a relaxed outing, 10 moderate trails that provide a good workout, and 5 difficult trails for experienced hikers seeking a challenge. This variety ensures there's something for everyone.

Q: Are there any trails that follow river paths?
A: Yes, many trails in La Jara are defined by their proximity to rivers. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1219443515/rio-pusa-from-los-navalucillos-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Pusa from Los Navalucillos — circular&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering a scenic route along the Pusa River. You'll find that river valleys are a common feature, contributing to the region's natural beauty.
tips: One of the characteristics of this town is its name which, as it indicates, shows us how the town is located next to a mountain pass, just like the pass itself.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Sierra de Altamira circular route from Puerto de San Vicente is classified as an easy hike. It's suitable for most fitness levels, with well-defined paths and a moderate duration, making it a great option for those new to hiking or looking for a less strenuous outdoor experience.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this hike?
faqs.answer: This circular route usually takes around 1 hour and 35 minutes to complete. This duration can vary depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain primarily features paths through beautiful Mediterranean forests, which are well-preserved. While generally easy, you'll experience some gentle ascents and descents, offering impressive 360-degree views of the surrounding Alcarria region from higher points.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints or historical sites along the way?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! The route starts from Puerto de San Vicente, which was historically a strategic point on an ancient Roman road. You can also look out for the abandoned Santa Quiteria Station nearby, offering a glimpse into the past. Ascending to higher points provides spectacular panoramic views of the entire Alcarria region.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife or natural features can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The well-preserved Mediterranean forest is rich in biodiversity. Keep an eye out for various bird species, including royal eagles, booted eagles, and griffon vultures. You might also spot different species of mustelids and ungulates, making it an interesting trail for nature observation.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Parking is generally available at or near the Puerto de San Vicente, which serves as the starting point for this circular route. It's advisable to arrive earlier on busy days to secure a spot.

faqs.question: Is the Sierra de Altamira circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially given the presence of wildlife and to ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn are particularly pleasant. During these seasons, the temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant, offering comfortable hiking conditions.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or is there an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits to hike this trail, and there are no entrance fees. It's freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other hiking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular route intersects with several other official trails. You'll find sections that are part of the Camino Natural de Las Villuercas, Camino de la Jara - Etapa 3, Camino de la Jara - Etapa 4, and Camino de los Montes de Toledo - Etapa 9, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or pubs near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While the immediate vicinity of Puerto de San Vicente is more natural, you may find small local establishments in nearby villages for refreshments after your hike. It's a good idea to bring your own water and snacks for the trail itself.
